title: QUIC RFC 9000 DPLPMTUD Probe Tracking Work Item
summary: Close the helper-backed RFC 9000 Section 14.3 DPLPMTUD subset by adding internal per-path probe tracking, PADDING probe sizing, BASE_PLPMTU initialization, and per-address-pair MPS isolation.
planned_changes:
- Add an internal DPLPMTUD state helper keyed by connection path identity.
- Track PMTU probe packet numbers until acknowledgment or loss.
- Promote per-path MPS only on acknowledged probes and keep losses non-promoting.
- Compute PADDING bytes for padding-based probes while requiring ack-eliciting payload bytes.
- Replace empty S14P3 requirement homes with positive, negative, edge, and fuzz coverage.
- Add a permanent BenchmarkDotNet suite for the helper's hot paths.
verification_plan: Run the focused S14P3 requirement-home tests, run the DPLPMTUD BenchmarkDotNet suite with Dry first, render/check the touched SpecTrace artifacts, and keep the verification scope limited to the internal helper-backed subset.
completion_notes: Implemented the internal DPLPMTUD state helper, replaced the four S14P3 placeholder requirement homes with executable proof coverage, and added a permanent BenchmarkDotNet suite for probe tracking and outcome processing.
